scope_note: QA Anchor Geodesic Separation cert [288]. Primary sources: Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (QA distance coordinates b=d(v,L)+1, e=d(v,R)+1); Cormen, T.H. et al. (2009) Introduction to Algorithms 3rd ed. MIT Press ISBN 978-0-262-03384-8 Ch.22 (BFS distance property: adjacent nodes differ by exactly 1). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): in any finite unweighted tree T with distinct anchor nodes L and R, for every edge (v,u), let Δb=d(u,L)-d(v,L) and Δe=d(u,R)-d(v,R). Then Δb*Δe = -1 iff edge (v,u) is on the unique L-R path P_LR; Δb*Δe = +1 iff edge (v,u) is NOT on P_LR. Proof: BFS gives Δb,Δe in {-1,+1} for all tree edges. On-path edges separate L from R on removal, forcing opposite signs. Off-path edges share a foot B on P_LR; moving away from B increases d(v,L) and d(v,R) simultaneously, forcing same signs. Corollary: qa_monotone_dir_score(v) = count of incident edges with Δb*Δe > 0. This equals 0 iff v is on P_LR and >= 1 iff v is off P_LR. Degree is naturally embedded: off-path interior score=2, off-path leaf score=1, on-path score=0. Theorem NT: all quantities are integer BFS distances; no float state. Mechanism chain: cert [279] (Orbit Access Theorem); benchmark qa_koenig_graph_anomaly_benchmark_2026-05-31c (AUROC=0.7779, AP=0.7803, top-k=0.8262). Lineage: derived 2026-05-31.
